pattern: Reversing each row and inverting every bit collapse into one pass: walk a row from both ends, swapping while flipping each value with 1 minus the bit.
hints: Two operations are requested, but they commute, so you can fold them into a single traversal of each row.
When you reverse a row, position i and its mirror position swap; while swapping you can also turn each 0 into a 1 and vice versa.
For each row use two indices closing in from the ends: swap the mirrored cells, replacing each with 1 minus its value; when a single middle cell remains just invert it in place.

edge_inputs.description: second statement example with even row width
single cell zero inverts to one
single cell one inverts to zero
all zeros become all ones regardless of reversal
odd width so the middle cell only inverts and does not move
palindromic row exposes whether reversal then inversion is applied

stress.description: random square binary images across allowed sizes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 20)
    image = [[rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [image]
stress.note: square matrices per the n x n constraint
